title: Bamboo Island Wildlife Sanctuary
tagline: Bamboo Island Wildlife Sanctuary — wildlife reserve of Andaman & Nicobar

heroImage.alt: Bamboo Island Wildlife Sanctuary, Andaman & Nicobar

overview.short: Bamboo Island Wildlife Sanctuary is a protected area located in the North and Middle Andaman District in the Indian Union territory of the Andaman and Nicobar Islands.
overview.description: Bamboo Island Wildlife Sanctuary is a protected area located in the North and Middle Andaman District in the Indian Union territory of the Andaman and Nicobar Islands. The sanctuary was officially established in 1987 and covers an area of just 0.05 km² (~5 hectares). It forms part of a network of small island wildlife sanctuaries in the Andaman island group aimed at preserving unique coastal ecosystems, including coral reefs, littoral forests, and bamboo groves.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Bamboo Island Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: The best time to visit Bamboo Island Wildlife Sanctuary is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Bamboo Island Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Veer Savarkar International Airport, Port Blair (158 km) and the nearest railway station is Puri Railway Station (1067 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Bamboo Island Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Bamboo Island Wildlife Sanctuary at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Bamboo Island Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: Stays in Bamboo Island Wildlife Sanctuary start from ₹2,500 per night, with options across 5 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Bamboo Island Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: The top attractions are Stewart Island (Andaman Islands), Curlew Island (Andaman Islands), Ramnagar, Diglipur, Stewart Sound Group.
